intro: Sarcomphalus lloydii, also known as Lloyd's sarcomphalus, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rhamnaceae. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Sarcomphalus lloydii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and immunomodulatory activities.
